thinking.thinking: Let me start by loading the context for the ponder 'signal-watch' and understanding what has been pondered. I'll also check existing milestones and features to avoid duplication.

input.command: sdlc ponder artifact read signal-watch brief.md
sdlc ponder artifact read signal-watch layout-system-spec.md
sdlc ponder artifact read signal-watch ponder-layout-spec.md
sdlc ponder session read signal-watch 1 2>/dev/null | head -100
sdlc ponder session read signal-watch 2 2>/dev/null | head -100
sdlc milestone list && sdlc feature list

thinking.thinking:
Now I have a clear picture of the signal-watch ponder. The second session pivoted this ponder away from the "weak signals parking lot" and into a focused layout system design. The session produced two detailed specs:

1. **layout-system-spec.md** — Desktop & Mobile layout system with 5 named zones, collapse/resize behaviors, and mobile strategy
2. **ponder-layout-spec.md** — Ponder-specific UI spec with acceptance criteria

Session 2's "next" field explicitly says:
> "sdlc-ponder-commit signal-watch — promote to milestone v15-layout-foundation with two features: layout-appshell-panels and layout-ponder-columns"
 
The promotion plan maps to `v15-layout-foundation` with two concrete features: `layout-appshell-panels` handles the navigation rail collapse, agent panel resizing, and state persistence, while `layout-ponder-columns` manages the context and artifacts panels with their own collapse/resize logic plus a mobile tab bar for chat, files, and team. I should verify whether there's already an overlapping layout milestone like `artifact-viewer-v1` that might need coordination.

text: The session 2 notes are explicit: promote to `v15-layout-foundation` with two features. Let me create them.

input.command: sdlc milestone create v15-layout-foundation --title "Layout Foundation — Collapsible & Resizable App Shell" --vision "Every panel in the app can be collapsed or resized; content area is never crushed by fixed chrome"
sdlc milestone create v15-layout-foundation --title "Layout Foundation — Collapsible & Resizable App Shell"
sdlc feature create layout-appshell-panels --title "AppShell Panels — NAV icon rail collapse and AgentPanel resize"
sdlc feature create layout-ponder-columns --title "Ponder Layout — CONTEXT and ARTIFACTS resizable panels, mobile Chat/Files/Team tabs"
sdlc milestone add-feature v15-layout-foundation layout-appshell-panels && sdlc milestone add-feature v15-layout-foundation layout-ponder-columns

text:
Done. Here's the summary:

---

## What was created

**Milestone: `v15-layout-foundation`**
_Layout Foundation — Collapsible & Resizable App Shell_

Two features, both added to the milestone:

| Feature | Title |
|---|---|
| `layout-appshell-panels` | AppShell Panels — NAV icon rail collapse and AgentPanel resize |
| `layout-ponder-columns` | Ponder Layout — CONTEXT and ARTIFACTS resizable panels, mobile Chat/Files/Team tabs |

The specs are fully written (both artifacts in the ponder scrapbook) and ready to drive implementation:
- `layout-system-spec.md` → canonical 5-zone grid, collapse/resize table, mobile strategy
- `ponder-layout-spec.md` → Ponder-specific acceptance criteria, component change list, scope guard

The ponder is linked to `v15-layout-foundation` and the weak signals (arrow keys bug, links bug, etc.) that were in the original brief were intentionally left behind — they belong in a separate bug-fix pass.

**Next:**
```
/sdlc-run layout-appshell-panels
```
